questions.text: A force which is experienced in a magnetic field depends on:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The force depends on the magnitude of the charge, as described in the formula F = qvB sin(θ).

options.text: speed of the moving charge v
options.explanation: The force depends on the velocity of the charge, as indicated in the formula F = qvB sin(θ).
The force depends on the magnetic field strength, as given by the formula F = qvB sin(θ).

The magnetic force on a charge is determined by the product of the charge magnitude, its velocity, and the magnetic field strength, as expressed in F = qvB sin(θ). Therefore, it depends on all these factors.
This is incorrect as the force does depend on the charge, velocity, and magnetic field strength, as per the formula F = qvB sin(θ).

questions.explanation: The force experienced by a charge moving in a magnetic field is given by the formula F = qvB sin(θ), where q is the charge, v is the velocity of the charge, and B is the magnetic field strength. The angle θ is the angle between the velocity vector and the magnetic field lines. Therefore, the force depends on the magnitude of the charge, the speed of the moving charge, and the strength of the magnetic field. Option D correctly identifies all these factors. Options A, B, and C each identify only one of these factors, which is insufficient on its own. Option E is incorrect as it suggests that the force depends on none of these factors, which is contrary to the established formula.

questions.text: An electron is moving along the axis of a solenoid carrying a current. Which of the following is a correct statement about the electromagnetic force acting on the electron?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: No Force will act on an electron, if there was some force then we have to decide its direction.

No Force will act on an electron, if there is some force then we have to decide its direction, As the electron is moving parallel 0° angle or antiparallel 180° with the magnetic field, in both cases, the value of sin is 0 making force 0.

options.text: The force acts in the direction of motion
options.explanation: Force always acts perpendicular to the direction of motion, if it acts according to Fleming left-hand rule.

In the given diagram, see the direction of magnetic field inside the solenoid, So if an electron moves through the axis , it will be parallel to the magnetic field. We know that for magnetic force to act , the moving charge must have must have perpendicular component , but electron is parallel , so no force will act Also , according to formulathere is an electron of charge q=1.6×10^−19C The electron is moving along the axis with velocity VForce = q(V x B)Velocity and magnetic field are in the same direction or at an angle of 180°V x B = VBsin(0° or 180°) = 0 (sin 0°=sin 180°=0)Force = 0The electron continues to move with the same velocity without getting disturbed.

questions.explanation: The question states along the axis of a solenoid. This implies that the Magnetic feild is parallel to the direction of current (For magnetic force to act, it is necessary that the direction of current and magentic feild are perpendicular to eachother) Therefore, no force acts and electron moves straight through without getting deflected.

questions.text: The magnetic field inside a long solenoid is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Varying with radial distance: This option is not correct. Inside a long solenoid, the magnetic field is essentially uniform along its central axis and does not vary with radial distance from the axis.

Uniform: The correct answer is this one. Inside a long solenoid, the magnetic field is indeed uniform. This means that the strength and direction of the magnetic field are the same at all points along the central axis of the solenoid.
Zero: This option is not correct. The magnetic field inside a long solenoid is not zero; it is nonzero and uniform.
Not enough Information: This option is not correct either. The information provided is sufficient to answer the question. Inside a long solenoid, the magnetic field is known to be uniform.

questions.explanation: The magnetic field inside a long straight solenoid-carrying current is the same at all points. It is because the magnetic field in the solenoid is constant because the lines are completely parallel to each other.As shown in figure, the magnetic field lines inside a long solenoid are straight lines, which represents uniform magnetic field.

questions.text: In an AC generator, AC reverses its direction _ times per second.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: 30: This option suggests that the AC current reverses its direction 30 times per second. However, this is not accurate.
40: This option suggests that the AC current reverses its direction 40 times per second. However, this is not accurate.

50: This option suggests that the AC current reverses its direction 50 times per second. This is the correct answer. AC current in many regions and power systems around the world is typically supplied at a frequency of 50 Hz, which means it changes direction (reverses) 50 times per second.

60: This option suggests that the AC current reverses its direction 60 times per second. While 60 Hz is also a common frequency for AC power in some regions, the most widely used standard frequency is 50 Hz.

questions.explanation: The frequency of an AC generator is 50 times per second.Hence, the current reverses its direction 50 times.

questions.text: Two parallel straight conductors, in which current is flowing in the same direction, attract each other. The cause of it is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Two parallel straight conductors carrying current in the same direction attract each other due to the magnetic force between them. This happens because:Magnetic Fields Around the Conductors:Each current-carrying conductor generates a circular magnetic field around it, as described by Ampère's right-hand rule.Magnetic Interaction:The magnetic field of one conductor exerts a force on the second conductor and vice versa.When currents are in the same direction, the magnetic fields between the wires reinforce each other in a way that causes an attractive force.Lorentz Force Explanation:The moving charges (current) in one conductor experience a force due to the magnetic field created by the other conductor.According to the Lorentz force law, this force acts inwards, pulling the conductors together.
Two parallel straight conductors carrying current in the same direction attract each other due to the magnetic force between them.
Two parallel straight conductors carrying current in the same direction attract each other due to the magnetic force between them.
Two parallel straight conductors carrying current in the same direction attract each other due to the magnetic force between them.

questions.explanation: Correct option is 'A'.

questions.text: A proton and an alpha particle, both are moving with same initial velocity, perpendicular to uniform magnetic field of 1 T. Find the ratio of radii of circles described by the alpha particle and the proton.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The radius of the circular path is proportional to the mass-to-charge ratio. The alpha particle has twice the charge and about four times the mass of a proton, resulting in a radius that is twice that of the proton.

This would imply that the radius of the alpha particle's path is half that of the proton's, which is incorrect because the alpha particle's larger mass increases the radius, despite its larger charge.
If the radii were equal, it would mean the mass-to-charge ratio of the proton and alpha particle is the same, which is not the case.
This would suggest the alpha particle's path radius is four times that of the proton's, which overestimates the effect of the mass increase relative to the increase in charge.

questions.explanation: The radius of a charged particle's path in a magnetic field is given by the formula r = (mv) / (qB), where m is the mass, v is the velocity, q is the charge, and B is the magnetic field strength. For a proton, m = m_p, q = e. For an alpha particle, m = 4m_p, q = 2e. Given the same velocity and magnetic field, the radius for the proton is r_p = (m_p * v) / (e * B) and for the alpha particle is r_alpha = (4m_p * v) / (2e * B). Simplifying, r_alpha / r_p = 2. Thus, the radius of the alpha particle's path is twice that of the proton's path. This makes Option A correct. Options B, C, and D are incorrect as they do not accurately reflect the differences in mass and charge between the proton and alpha particle.

questions.text: A proton and an electron both moving with the same velocity v enter into a region of magnetic field directed perpendicular to the velocity of the particles. They will now move in circular orbits such that

options.text: The time period for the electron will be higher

options.text: The time period for the proton will be higher
options.explanation: The time period T is given by the formula T = 2πm/qB. Since the proton has a greater mass than the electron, its time period will be longer.

options.text: Their orbital radii will be the same
options.explanation: The radius of the orbit is influenced by the mass and charge of the particle, so they will not be the same.

questions.explanation: Option B is correctWe know that period T= qB/2πm ​ i.e., T∝m (Since q and B are same) ∵ Mass of proton > Mass of electron ∴ Time period of proton > Time period of electron.

A helix is a three-dimensional curve characterized by a circular path combined with a linear motion along an axis. While the particle's motion can have a component in the direction of the magnetic field, the trajectory is not a helix because the particle does not exhibit a linear motion along an axis.
A parabola is a U-shaped curve. The trajectory of a charged particle projected perpendicularly into a magnetic field is not a parabola since it does not follow a U-shaped path.
The correct answer circular because a charged particle projected perpendicularly into a magnetic field moves in a circular trajectory due to the perpendicular Lorentz force acting on it.

questions.text: A homogeneous electric field E and a uniform magnetic field B are pointing in the same direction. A proton is projected with its velocity parallel to E. It will:

options.text: Go on moving in the same direction with increasing velocity
options.explanation: This option suggests that the proton will continue to move in the same direction as the initial velocity (parallel to the electric field), and its speed will increase over time. In other words, the proton will accelerate in the direction of the electric field.

options.text: Go on moving in the same direction with constant velocity
options.explanation: This option states that the proton will maintain a constant velocity in the same direction as its initial velocity (parallel to the electric field). In this case, there would be no acceleration or deceleration; the proton's speed would remain unchanged.

This option implies that the proton will experience a force or change in direction that causes it to turn to the right with respect to its initial velocity direction (the direction of the electric field). This would happen due to the interaction between the electric field and the magnetic field acting on the charged particle.

Again, this would happen due to the interaction between the electric field and the magnetic field acting on the charged particle.

questions.explanation: The proton will continue to move in the same direction as the initial velocity (parallel to the electric field), and its speed will increase over time. In other words, the proton will accelerate in the direction of the electric field.

questions.text: Ampere's law give us the relationship between:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option does not relate to Ampere's law. Ampere's law is about the relationship between current and magnetic fields, not forces or velocities of charges.
This option relates to the Lorentz force law, not Ampere's law. The Lorentz force law describes the force experienced by a charged particle in a magnetic field.

This option does not directly relate to Ampere's law. While current can create a magnetic field, Ampere's law specifically addresses the relationship between current and the resulting magnetic field, not current and force.

Ampere's law is a fundamental law in electromagnetism that relates the circulation of the magnetic field around a closed loop to the current passing through the loop. It states that the line integral of the magnetic field (B) around a closed loop is equal to the product of the current (I) passing through the loop and a constant called the permeability of free space (μ₀). This law establishes a relationship between the current (I) and the magnetic field (B) produced by that current.

questions.explanation: Ampere's law is a fundamental law in electromagnetism that relates the circulation of the magnetic field around a closed loop to the current passing through the loop. It states that the line integral of the magnetic field (B) around a closed loop is equal to the product of the current (I) passing through the loop and a constant called the permeability of free space (μ₀). This law establishes a relationship between the current (I) and the magnetic field (B) produced by that current.

questions.text: What is the magnetic force on a stationary charged particle in a uniform magnetic field?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A stationary charged particle in a uniform magnetic field experiences no magnetic force. This is because the magnetic force on a charged particle is determined by its velocity (v) and the angle (θ) between the velocity and the magnetic field (B) according to the formula:F = qvBsinθIf the particle is stationary (meaning its velocity v is zero), then sinθ is also zero, and as a result, the magnetic force becomes zero (F = 0).So, for a stationary charged particle in a uniform magnetic field, the magnetic force is zero.

A stationary charged particle in a uniform magnetic field experiences no magnetic force. This is because the magnetic force on a charged particle is determined by its velocity (v) and the angle (θ) between the velocity and the magnetic field (B) according to the formula:F = qvBsinθIf the particle is stationary (meaning its velocity v is zero), then sinθ is also zero, and as a result, the magnetic force becomes zero (F = 0).So, for a stationary charged particle in a uniform magnetic field, the magnetic force is zero.

This is because the magnetic force on a charged particle is determined by its velocity (v) and the angle (θ) between the velocity and the magnetic field (B) according to the formula:F = qvBsinθIf the particle is stationary (meaning its velocity v is zero), then sinθ is also zero, and as a result, the magnetic force becomes zero (F = 0).So, for a stationary charged particle in a uniform magnetic field, the magnetic force is zero.

A stationary charged particle in a uniform magnetic field experiences no magnetic force. This is because the magnetic force on a charged particle is determined by its velocity (v) and the angle (θ) between the velocity and the magnetic field (B) according to the formula:F = qvBsinθIf the particle is stationary (meaning its velocity v is zero), then sinθ is also zero, and as a result, the magnetic force becomes zero (F = 0).So, for a stationary charged particle in a uniform magnetic field, the magnetic force is zero.

questions.text: A moving charge is surrounded by:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is correct. A moving charge is surrounded by both electric and magnetic fields, resulting in a total of two fields.
This option is incorrect. A moving charge is surrounded by both electric and magnetic fields, resulting in a total of two fields.
This option is incorrect. A moving charge is surrounded by both electric and magnetic fields, resulting in a total of two fields.
This option is incorrect. A moving charge is surrounded by both electric and magnetic fields, resulting in a total of two fields.

questions.explanation: A moving charge, or an electric charge in motion, generates two types of fields:1. Electric Field (E): An electric field is produced by a stationary charge or a moving charge. It is a vector field that exerts a force on other charged objects. The electric field extends in all directions around the charge, and its strength depends on the magnitude of the charge and the distance from the charge.2. Magnetic Field (B): A magnetic field is generated by a moving charge or a current-carrying wire. It is also a vector field and exerts forces on other moving charges or current-carrying wires. The magnetic field forms loops around the direction of the current flow, and its strength depends on the current and the distance from the current-carrying wire.So, a moving charge is surrounded by both electric and magnetic fields, resulting in a total of two fields.

questions.text: An electron moves at 2x102 m/s perpendicular to a magnetic field of 2T. What is the magnitude of magnetic force?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect as per the formula.

This option is correct. To calculate the magnitude of the magnetic force experienced by an electron moving perpendicular to a magnetic field, you can use the formula for the magnetic force on a charged particle: F = |q| * v * B Where: F is the magnitude of the magnetic force (in Newtons, N). |q| is the absolute value of the charge of the electron (which is approximately 1.602 x 10^-19 C, the elementary charge of an electron). v is the magnitude of the velocity of the electron (in meters per second, m/s). B is the magnitude of the magnetic field (in Tesla, T). Given: v = 2 x 10^2 m/s B = 2 T |q| ≈ 1.602 x 10^-19 C Now, plug in the values: F = |q| * v * B F = (1.602 x 10^-19 C) * (2 x 10^2 m/s) * (2 T) F ≈ 6.408 x 10^-17 N After calculating the expression, the magnitude of the magnetic force on the electron is approximately 6.408 x 10^-17 N.
This option is incorrect as per the formula.
This option is incorrect as per the formula.

B is the magnitude of the magnetic field (in Tesla, T). Given: v = 2 x 10^2 m/s B = 2 T |q| ≈ 1.602 x 10^-19 C Now, plug in the values: F = |q| * v * B F = (1.602 x 10^-19 C) * (2 x 10^2 m/s) * (2 T) F ≈ 6.408 x 10^-17 N After calculating the expression, the magnitude of the magnetic force on the electron is approximately 6.408 x 10^-17 N.

questions.text: A metal rod of length 1 m is moving at a speed of 1m/s in the direction making to an angle of 30o with 0.5 T magnetic field. The emf produced is:

questions.explanation: The induced EMF (E) in a moving conductor within a magnetic field is calculated using the formula: E = B * l * v * sin(θ), where:B is the magnetic field strength (0.5 T)l is the length of the rod (1 m)v is the velocity of the rod (1 m/s)θ is the angle between the direction of velocity and the magnetic field (30o)Substituting these values, we have:E = 0.5 * 1 * 1 * sin(30o) = 0.5 * 1 * 1 * 0.5 = 0.25VTherefore, the correct answer is 0.25V. questions.explanation: Explanation is given below:

questions.text: The mutual induction happens in?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: AC generator generates electrical power through the principles of electromagnetic induction, but it does not involve mutual induction. In a generator, a rotating coil or magnet generates an alternating current by inducing a voltage in the coil or wire. However, this is a self-induction effect, where the changing magnetic field in the coil induces a voltage in the same coil.

DC generator generates electrical power through the principles of electromagnetic induction, but it does not involve mutual induction. In a generator, a rotating coil or magnet generates an alternating current by inducing a voltage in the coil or wire. However, this is a self-induction effect, where the changing magnetic field in the coil induces a voltage in the same coil.
Battery is a device that converts chemical energy into electrical energy and does not involve induction.
Transformers work on the principle of mutual induction. Mutual Induction is a phenomenon in which changing current in one coil induces an e.m.f. in another coil.

questions.explanation: Option A: AC generator generates electrical power through the principles of electromagnetic induction, but it does not involve mutual induction. In a generator, a rotating coil or magnet generates an alternating current by inducing a voltage in the coil or wire. However, this is a self-induction effect, where the changing magnetic field in the coil induces a voltage in the same coil. Option B: DC generator generates electrical power through the principles of electromagnetic induction, but it does not involve mutual induction. In a generator, a rotating coil or magnet generates an alternating current by inducing a voltage in the coil or wire. However, this is a self-induction effect, where the changing magnetic field in the coil induces a voltage in the same coil. Option C: A battery is a device that converts chemical energy into electrical energy and does not involve induction. Option D: Transformers work on the principle of mutual induction. Mutual Induction is a phenomenon in which changing current in one coil induces an e.m.f. in another coil.

questions.text: Principle of transformer is ?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The principle of a transformer is mutual inductance. Mutual inductance is the phenomenon in which a changing current in one coil induces a voltage in another coil that is physically near it. A transformer consists of two coils of wire that are wound around a common core. When an alternating current flows through the primary coil, it creates a changing magnetic field. This changing magnetic field induces a voltage in the secondary coil. The amount of voltage induced in the secondary coil is proportional to the number of turns in the secondary coil and the strength of the magnetic field. By adjusting the number of turns in the primary and secondary coils, a transformer can be used to increase or decrease the voltage of an alternating current. This makes transformers very useful for transmitting electric power over long distances.
Self-induction is the phenomenon in which a changing current in a coil induces a voltage in the same coil. This is not the principle of a transformer.
Motional emf is the voltage that is induced in a conductor when it moves through a magnetic field. This is not the principle of a transformer.
None of these is also incorrect because the principle of a transformer is mutual inductance.

questions.explanation: Correct option is A. Principle of transformer is Mutual Induction. When a transformer primary has alternating current flowing in the conductor, magnetic flux surrounds the conductor in proportion to the amount of the current. The expanding and contracting flux cuts the conductors in the secondary and induces a voltage in the secondary. A transformer is an electrical apparatus designed to convert alternating current from one voltage to another. It can be designed to "step up" or "step down" voltages and works on the magnetic induction principle.

questions.text: Mutual inductance has a practical role in the performance of?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: AC generators are based on the principle of electromagnetic induction. When a coil is rotated about an axis perpendicular to the direction of uniform magnetic field, an induced emf is produced across it.AC generators are based on the principle of electromagnetic induction. When a coil is rotated about an axis perpendicular to the direction of uniform magnetic field, an induced emf is produced across it.
A choke is used in radio frequency circuits because it opposes any change, i.e. the radio frequency signal, but allows any steady, i.e. DC current to flow.

A DC generator operates on the principle of Faraday's laws of electromagnetic induction. According to Faraday's law, whenever a conductor is placed in a fluctuating magnetic field (or when a conductor is moved in a magnetic field) an EMF is induced in the conductor.

A transformer is a device constructed of two or more coils in close proximity to each other, with the express purpose of creating a condition of mutual inductance between the coils. A transformer takes High Voltage electricity with a small current and changes it into low voltage electricity with the large current, or vice versa. That is, step up or step down the voltage in a circuit . A Step Up Transformer turns low voltage electricity into High Voltage electricity while dropping the current. A step down Transformer changes High Voltage electricity into low voltage electricity. Hence mutual induction is the principle of a transformer.

questions.explanation: Mutual inductance is where the magnetic field generated by a coil of wire induces a voltage in an adjacent coil of wire. A transformer is a device constructed of two or more coils in close proximity to each other, with the express purpose of creating a condition of mutual inductance between the coils. A transformer takes high-voltage electricity with a small current and changes it into low-voltage electricity with a large current, or vice versa. That is, step up or step down the voltage in a circuit. A step-up transformer turns low-voltage electricity into high-voltage electricity while dropping the current. A step-down transformer changes high-voltage electricity into low-voltage electricity. Hence, mutual induction is the principle of a "transformer".

questions.text: The SI unit of magnetic induction is _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: "Weber": The Weber (Wb) is the SI unit of magnetic flux.
"Gauss": The Gauss (G) is a non-SI unit of magnetic induction. It is equal to 1/10,000th of a Tesla.
The correct answer is "Tesla."
"Maxwell": The Maxwell (Mx) is a non-SI unit of magnetic flux, similar to the Weber.

questions.explanation: The tesla (symbol: T) is a derived unit of the magnetic B-field strength (also called magnetic flux density) in the International System of Units. One Tesla is equal to one Weber per square meter.

questions.text: Faraday's law explains how electric field will interact with:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Faraday's law does not explain how electric fields interact with each other. It only explains how a changing magnetic field can induce an electric field. The electric field will interact with charged particles, such as electrons, causing them to move and generating an electric current.

Faraday's law is one of the fundamental principles of electromagnetism. It states that a changing magnetic field will induce an electric field. This means that if you have a magnetic field that is changing over time, it will create an electric field that is also changing over time. The electric field will interact with charged particles, such as electrons, causing them to move. This movement of charged particles generates an electric current. This principle is used in many devices, including generators, transformers, and motors. For example, a generator uses Faraday's law to convert mechanical energy into electrical energy. When a coil of wire is rotated in a magnetic field, the changing magnetic field induces an electric field in the coil, which generates an electric current. This current can be used to power electrical devices.

Faraday's law does not explain how an electric field will interact with a battery. However, an electric field can affect the behavior of charged particles in a battery, which can affect the battery's performance. For example, if an electric field is applied to a battery, it can cause the charged particles in the battery to move, which can affect the battery's voltage and current output.
This option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Faraday's law is one of the fundamental principles of electromagnetism. It states that a changing magnetic field will induce an electric field. This means that if you have a magnetic field that is changing over time, it will create an electric field that is also changing over time. The electric field will interact with charged particles, such as electrons, causing them to move. This movement of charged particles generates an electric current. This principle is used in many devices, including generators, transformers, and motors. For example, a generator uses Faraday's law to convert mechanical energy into electrical energy. When a coil of wire is rotated in a magnetic field, the changing magnetic field induces an electric field in the coil, which generates an electric current. This current can be used to power electrical devices.

questions.text: A copper ring is held horizontally and a bar magnet is dropped through the ring with its length along the axis of the ring. The acceleration of the falling magnet is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Equal to that due to gravity
options.explanation: The acceleration of the falling magnet is less than "g"

options.text: Less than that due to gravity
options.explanation: When a bar magnet is dropped through the ring, according to Lenz's law, the direction of the induced current will decrease (oppose) the motion of the freely falling bar magnet. So, the acceleration of the bar magnet is less than "g".

options.text: More than that due to gravity
options.explanation: The acceleration of the falling magnet is less than "g"

options.text: Depends on the diameter of the ring and the length of the magnet
options.explanation: The acceleration of the falling magnet is less than "g", and does not depend on the diameter of the ring and the length of the magnet.

questions.explanation: The explanation is: When a bar magnet is dropped through a copper ring with its length along the axis of the ring, it induces eddy currents in the ring. These eddy currents create a magnetic field that opposes the motion of the falling magnet due to Lenz's law. As a result, the falling magnet experiences a magnetic damping force. This magnetic damping force acts against the force of gravity, and it effectively reduces the acceleration of the falling magnet. The magnet is not in free fall because it is experiencing this additional magnetic force, making its acceleration less than that due to gravity. So, option b is the correct answer. The acceleration of the falling magnet is less than that due to gravity due to the opposing magnetic force generated by the ring.

questions.text: A transformer is used to step up an alternating emf of 220 V to 4.4 kV in order to transmit 6.6 kW of power. If the primary coil has 100 turns, what is the number of turns in the secondary?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect as per the formula. Np/Ns = Vp/Vs 100/Ns = 220/4400 100/Ns = 1/20 Ns = 2000

This option is correct. To determine the number of turns in the secondary coil of the transformer, we can use the turns ratio formula, which relates the primary and secondary voltages and the number of turns in each coil. The formula is as follows: Turns ratio (Np/Ns) = Voltage ratio (Vp/Vs) Given information: Primary voltage (Vp) = 220 V Secondary voltage (Vs) = 4.4 kV = 4400 V Number of turns in the primary coil (Np) = 100 Using the turns ratio formula: Np/Ns = Vp/Vs Substituting the known values: 100/Ns = 220/4400 Simplifying the equation: 100/Ns = 1/20 Cross-multiplying: Ns = 100 * 20 Ns = 2000 Therefore, the number of turns in the secondary coil is 2,000.
This option is incorrect as per the formula. Np/Ns = Vp/Vs 100/Ns = 220/4400 100/Ns = 1/20 Ns = 2000
This option is incorrect as per the formula. Np/Ns = Vp/Vs 100/Ns = 220/4400 100/Ns = 1/20 Ns = 2000

questions.text: In the following figure, a metallic ring is shown to fall vertically on a bar magnet. The induced current in the ring will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: There is a change in magnetic flux as the ring falls, which induces a current. Hence, the current cannot be zero.

The induced current creates a magnetic field that opposes the change in magnetic flux due to Lenz's law. As the ring falls, the direction of the induced current will be clockwise to oppose the increase in magnetic flux.

By Lenz's law, the induced current should oppose the increase in magnetic flux. The current will be in the clockwise direction, not counter clockwise, to create a magnetic field opposing the magnet's field.

options.text: Along the direction of the fall
options.explanation: The current direction is not related to the physical motion of the ring, but rather to the opposition of magnetic flux changes. Therefore, it cannot be along the direction of the fall.

questions.explanation: As the metallic ring falls towards the bar magnet, it experiences a change in magnetic flux, which induces a current in the ring. According to Lenz's law, the direction of the induced current will be such that it opposes the change in flux. This means the induced current will create its own magnetic field to counteract the increase in magnetic field from the bar magnet as the ring approaches it. By applying the right-hand rule, we determine that this induced current will flow in a clockwise direction when viewed from above. The other options are incorrect because they either do not account for the change in magnetic flux or incorrectly determine the direction of the induced current.

questions.text: A magnet is introduced into the coil and a voltage is induced across the coil. Which of the following factor has no effect on the induced votage?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Thickness of the wire of the coil
options.explanation: The thickness (diameter) of the wire in a coil can have an effect on the induced voltage, although it might not be a primary factor. The induced voltage in a coil is mainly determined by the rate of change of magnetic flux through the coil, which is influenced by factors like the strength of the magnetic field, the speed of the magnetic field change, the number of turns in the coil, and the orientation of the coil. The thickness of the wire affects the coil's resistance and self-inductance. Thicker wires generally have lower resistance, which means that the induced current due to the induced voltage might be larger in a coil with thicker wire. Additionally, the thickness of the wire could influence the coil's self-inductance, affecting how the coil reacts to changes in current. However, the thickness of the wire is not usually a primary factor in determining the induced voltage compared to factors like the rate of change of magnetic flux or the strength of the magnetic field.

options.text: The time in which magnet is introduced
options.explanation: The time in which a magnet is introduced into a coil does not affect the induced voltage. The induced voltage in a coil is determined by the rate of change of magnetic flux through the coil, which is influenced by factors such as the strength of the magnetic field, the speed of the magnet's movement, and the number of turns in the coil. The specific time at which the magnet is introduced does not directly impact the induced voltage, as long as the rate of change of magnetic flux remains the same. The induced voltage is a result of how quickly the magnetic flux changes, not the exact moment at which the magnet is introduced.

options.text: The strength of the magnetic field
options.explanation: The strength of the magnetic field does have an effect on the induced voltage in a coil. According to Faraday's law of electromagnetic induction, the induced voltage (emf) in a coil is directly proportional to the rate of change of magnetic flux through the coil. The magnetic flux is affected by the strength of the magnetic field, among other factors. If the strength of the magnetic field increases, assuming other factors remain constant, the rate of change of magnetic flux will increase as well. This, in turn, will lead to a higher induced voltage. So, the strength of the magnetic field does indeed affect the induced voltage in a coil.

options.text: Number of turns of the coil
options.explanation: The number of turns in the coil does indeed have an effect on the induced voltage. According to Faraday's law of electromagnetic induction, the induced voltage (emf) in a coil is directly proportional to the rate of change of magnetic flux through the coil. The magnetic flux is influenced by factors including the strength of the magnetic field and the number of turns in the coil. Specifically, if the number of turns in the coil increases, the induced voltage will also increase for the same rate of change of magnetic flux. This is because each turn of the coil contributes to the overall flux linkage, leading to a higher induced voltage. So, the number of turns of the coil does have an effect on the induced voltage.

questions.explanation: The time in which a magnet is introduced into a coil does not affect the induced voltage. The induced voltage in a coil is determined by the rate of change of magnetic flux through the coil, which is influenced by factors such as the strength of the magnetic field, the speed of the magnet's movement, and the number of turns in the coil. The specific time at which the magnet is introduced does not directly impact the induced voltage, as long as the rate of change of magnetic flux remains the same. The induced voltage is a result of how quickly the magnetic flux changes, not the exact moment at which the magnet is introduced.

questions.text: A generator supplies 100 V to the primary coil of a transformer. The primary has 50 turns and the secondary has 500 turns. The secondary voltage is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is correct. The secondary voltage is calculated using the transformer formula: Vp/Vs = Np/Ns, where Vp is the primary voltage, Vs is the secondary voltage, Np is the number of turns in the primary coil, and Ns is the number of turns in the secondary coil. Given Vp = 100V, Np = 50, and Ns = 500, the secondary voltage Vs = (Ns/Np) * Vp = (500/50) * 100V = 1000V.
This option is incorrect. It results from a misunderstanding of the transformer formula. The correct formula to calculate the secondary voltage is Vs = (Ns/Np) * Vp, not Vs = (Vp/Ns) * Np.
This option is incorrect. It results from incorrectly applying the transformer formula. The number of turns and voltage ratios are not correctly applied to arrive at this value.
This option is incorrect. This value assumes no transformation, which is not the case here as the number of turns in the primary and secondary coils are different.

questions.explanation: Solve by using the formula Ns/Np=Vs/Vp

questions.text: The induced emf does not depend on:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The induced emf is directly proportional to the area of the coil. A larger coil will capture more magnetic flux lines, resulting in a greater change in flux and a higher induced emf. So, the induced emf does depend on the area of the coil.

Increasing the number of turns in the coil enhances the emf. This is because each turn contributes to the change in flux, and more turns mean a larger contribution to the total induced emf. Therefore, the induced emf depends on the number of turns of the coil.

The resistance of the coil affects the current that flows due to the induced emf, but it doesn't directly affect the emf itself. The induced emf is determined by the changing magnetic flux, not the coil's resistance. So, the induced emf does not depend on the resistance of the coil.

The length of the coil can indirectly affect the induced emf. A longer coil may result in a slower change in magnetic flux for a given situation, but the emf depends on the change in magnetic flux, not the coil's length. So, it's not accurate to say that the induced emf depends on the length of the coil.

questions.explanation: Induced emf does not depend upon the resistance or circuit. It depends upon the time rate of change of magnetic flux. According to Farady’s law:

questions.text: The correct relation for transformer is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This relation is incorrect. It does not represent the correct relationship between the number of turns in thesecondary coil (Ns), the number of turns in the primary coil (Np), the secondary current (Is), and the primary current (Ip). The correct relationship involves the ratio of turns to the ratio of currents.
This relation is incorrect. While it involves the ratios of turns and currents, it does not correctly represent the relationship between these quantities in a transformer.
This relation is incorrect. While it involves the ratios of turns and currents, it does not correctly represent the relationship between these quantities in a transformer.

Option "d" (Ns/Np = Ip/Is) is correct because it accurately represents the relationship between the turns ratio and the current ratio in a transformer. The turns ratio (Ns/Np) indicates how many times the secondary voltage differs from the primary voltage, and it is proportional to the current ratio (Ip/Is). This means that when the number of turns in the secondary coil (Ns) is greater than in the primary coil (Np), it's a step-up transformer, and the secondary current (Is) is smaller than the primary current (Ip). Conversely, for a step-down transformer, where Ns is less than Np, the secondary current (Is) is greater than the primary current (Ip). This relationship is a fundamental aspect of transformer operation.

questions.text: Each sentence below has a blank indicating that something has been omitted. Choose the word that best fits in with the meaning of the sentence as a whole.Abid’s _ for learning history should prove to be practical during his studies to become a history teacher.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: "Disregard" means lack of consideration or attention. This does not fit because a lack of interest in learning history would not be practical for becoming a history teacher.

"Penchant" means a strong or habitual liking for something or tendency to do something. This fits well because having a strong interest in learning history would indeed be practical for becoming a history teacher.
"Abhorrence" means a feeling of repulsion or strong hatred. This does not fit because having a strong dislike for learning history would not be practical for becoming a history teacher.
"Empathy" means the ability to understand and share the feelings of others. While empathy is a good trait for a teacher, it does not specifically relate to an interest in learning history.

questions.explanation: To have a "penchant" for something means to have a fondness for it. A fondness for history would be practical or helpful for future studies in the field. Answer choices A and C are incorrect because anyone having "disregard" or "Empathy" for history would certainly not study to become a history teacher.

questions.text: Spot the error. When my sister was a little girl she asked my mother and Uncle Bill the name of a certain old lady.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: This conjunction is correctly used to introduce a time clause.
This adverbial phrase is correctly used to describe the age of the sister when she asks the question.
The error in the sentence is in option (C) "my mother," which should be corrected to "our mother" for grammatical accuracy and clarity.
This determiner is correctly used to specify a particular old lady whose name was asked about.

questions.explanation: The error in the sentence is in option (C) "my mother," which should be corrected to "our mother" for grammatical accuracy and clarity.

questions.text: Each sentence below has a blank indicating that something has been omitted. Choose the word that best fits in with the meaning of the sentence as a whole.Azhar loves roses for the_ appeal of their petals and leaves, while I am most _ by their olfactory properties.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: "Aesthetic" refers to the appreciation of beauty, which makes sense for the appeal of petals and leaves. "Enthralled" means captivated or fascinated, which fits well with being drawn to their smell.

"Acerbic" means sharp or biting, often used to describe taste or tone rather than visual appeal. "Fascinated" fits the context of being drawn to their smell, but "acerbic" does not fit the first blank.

"Pictorial" means relating to pictures, which could describe visual appeal but not as fittingly as "aesthetic." "Dissatisfied" means not pleased or content, which doesn't fit with being drawn to the smell of roses.

"Implicit" means implied rather than expressly stated, which doesn't fit the context of visual appeal. "Disenchanted" means disappointed or no longer fascinated, which also doesn't fit the context of being drawn to the smell.

questions.explanation: Because the sentence indicates that Azhar "loves roses," the word that best fits in the first blank should have a positive connotation. The first word in answer choice B has a negative connotation, and the first word in answer choice has a neutral connotation. “Aesthetic" refers to "the appreciation of beauty," which makes the most sense in this sentence. Likewise,"enthralled," which means "captivated," works well in the second blank.

questions.text: In this section of logical deduction, the question consists of a brief passage (usually a report containing certain data regarding some social or economic problem), followed by certain inferences based on it. The student is required to analyze the contents (or data) of the passage and grasp the desirable facts from it. Then, student has to consider each of the given inferences in the context of the given passage. decide upon its degree of truth or falsity and then choose the hest alternative provided accordingly.Though the State cultivates only 3.2 lakh tons of mangoes. they are of premium quality and with mangoes becoming second most consumed fruit in the world after grapes, the government has been trying exporting it through sea route which is cheaper. An experiment which was done in this regard last year hasred successful.The State also exports good quality grapes.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: The inference that the State exports mangoes through the sea route is supported by the passage, but the inference about exporting only premium quality mangoes is false. Hence, this option is incorrect because not all given inferences are definitely true.
This is not correct.

Step 1: Key facts from the passageThe State cultivates 3.2 lakh tons of mangoes.The mangoes are of premium quality.Mangoes are the second most consumed fruit in the world after grapes.The government has been trying to export mangoes by sea route (cheaper) and last year’s experiment was successful.Step 2: Given inference“The State also exports good quality grapes.”Step 3: VerificationThe passage only says the State cultivates mangoes and is trying to export them.It also mentions that grapes are the most consumed fruit in the world, but this is a global fact, not specific to the State.Nowhere in the passage is it stated that the State exports grapes or even grows them.Thus, this inference is not supported by the given passage.✅ Final AnswerThe inference “The State also exports good quality grapes” is False / Not supported by the passage.

While the passage refutes the inference about exporting only premium quality mangoes, it supports the inference regarding the sea route. Therefore, this option does not accurately reflect the information provided.
Since the passage confirms the sea route export, this option is incorrect because not all inferences are false.

questions.explanation: Step 1: Key facts from the passageThe State cultivates 3.2 lakh tons of mangoes.The mangoes are of premium quality.Mangoes are the second most consumed fruit in the world after grapes.The government has been trying to export mangoes by sea route (cheaper) and last year’s experiment was successful.Step 2: Given inference“The State also exports good quality grapes.”Step 3: VerificationThe passage only says the State cultivates mangoes and is trying to export them.It also mentions that grapes are the most consumed fruit in the world, but this is a global fact, not specific to the State.Nowhere in the passage is it stated that the State exports grapes or even grows them.Thus, this inference is not supported by the given passage.✅ Final AnswerThe inference “The State also exports good quality grapes” is False / Not supported by the passage.

questions.text: In the following question, two statements numbered I and II are given. There may be cause and effect relationship between the two statements. These two statements may be the effect of the same cause or independent causes. These statements may be independent causes without saving any relationship. Read both the statements in question and mark your answer as:A. If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect;B. If statement II is the cause and statement, I is its effect;C. If both the statements I and II are independent causes;D. If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes;E. If both the statements I and II are effects of some common causes;I. Past few months, Babar is behaving abnormally.II. Babar's father had taken him to a doctor yesterday.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: A. If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect;
options.explanation: Statement I suggests that Babar's abnormal behavior prompted his father to take him to the doctor, which is described in statement II.

options.text: B. If statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect;
options.explanation: This would imply that Babar's visit to the doctor caused his abnormal behavior, which is not logical given the sequence of the events described.

options.text: C. If both the statements I and II are independent causes;
options.explanation: This option suggests that both statements are causes of different effects, which does not apply here as they are linked.

options.text: D. If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes:
options.explanation: This would mean each statement is the result of different causes, but the context suggests a direct cause-effect relationship between the two.

options.text: E. If both the statements I and II are effects of some common cause;
options.explanation: This implies both statements result from the same cause, but the logical connection indicates that one statement is the cause of the other.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Babar's abnormal behavior (Statement I) is likely the reason his father decided to take him to a doctor (Statement II). This implies a cause-effect relationship where Statement I is the cause, and Statement II is the effect. Other options are incorrect as they either misinterpret the cause-effect sequence or suggest independence that doesn't exist in the context provided.
